mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals an ongoing expansion in labor demand that supports wage income and consumption spending, lifting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of month-over-month PAYEMS changes with 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate above 5.0% restricts credit availability, raises corporate borrowing costs, and compresses equity valuation multiples through a higher discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of declining industrial production and an elevated federal funds rate creates a joint drag on corporate profitability and equity valuations that is not captured by either series in isolation, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Bivariate interaction term of INDPRO change and FEDFUNDS level is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in predicting 60-day returns over a 2-year rolling window, or the interaction model underperforms the best univariate model by more than 0.05 AUC.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real retail sales predict negative S&P 500 returns specifically during late-cycle expansions when consumer spending weakness at full employment triggers rapid earnings downgrades, with the effect absent in early-recovery regimes.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight monetary policy
failure_condition: RSAFS declines do not produce negative 20-day returns during months when UNRATE is below 5% and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4% over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in housing starts lead residential construction and durable goods orders lower, creating a persistent drag on aggregate demand and corporate earnings that generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month changes show a Rank IC greater than -0.02 with 60-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Headline CPI inflation exceeding a 3-month threshold of 1.5 index points forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive monetary policy, raising real discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are non-negative in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL 3-month change exceeds 1.5 index points.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of rising unemployment and falling retail sales creates a negative consumption-income feedback loop that is not captured by either series in isolation, generating strongly negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of UNRATE and RSAFS changes does not significantly improve prediction of 20-day returns (F-test p > 0.05) relative to additive univariate models over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly declines in real GDP signal a contraction in aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenues across sectors and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 declines fail to predict negative 60-day forward returns with Rank IC below -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
